profile.name: Vanessa Asafo-Adjei
profile.aboutBio:
I'm Vanessa — a Ghanaian game developer, narrative designer, and creative at heart who loves building games that tell stories.
I recently wrapped up my National Service at LetiArts, one of Africa's leading game studios, where I shipped several titles, led a full game project, and taught game development to kids. I'm now a Unity Developer at Organised Khaos, where I continue to build and grow.
profile.aboutBody:
Some highlights from my work: I built the Relax Mode for LetiArts' mobile game Sweave, and led Dial of Truth as Technical Lead — a cozy investigative desktop game celebrating modern African culture — handling everything from Unity/C# development and system design to narrative writing and team coordination. I also built HideAway, a web game that quietly racked up over 23,000 play sessions on MTN's Ayoba platform.
What makes me a little different is that I genuinely love all sides of the process. I care deeply about writing — crafting characters, building worlds, and finding the story underneath the game. I get excited about design, the way mechanics and visuals come together to create a feeling. And I love art, from pixel art and 2D illustrations to the assets that bring a game's world to life. For me, code is just another creative tool.
I work across C#, C++, Unity, narrative design, and 2D digital art — and I'm always most in my element when storytelling, design, and technology collide.

projects.title: Starfall Chronicles
projects.description: A narrative pixel-art RPG about a star that fell to earth. Built in Godot in 3 months.
